sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 3-day history of a hacking cough, which started 3 days ago after being around her sister who has similar symptoms.
The cough has been gradually worsening, with the production of green phlegm.
The patient also reports a sore throat, nasal congestion, and no fever, shortness of breath, body aches, nausea, or vomiting.
The patient has a history of seasonal allergies, specifically sneezing and runny nose in the spring.
She has been managing her diabetes with metformin 500mg daily, with good blood sugar control (range of 120).
The patient has been experiencing elevated blood pressure, with readings of 144/72, and is taking hydrochlorothiazide 25mg daily.
Past medical history: The patient has type 2 diabetes, for which she is taking metformin 500mg daily.
She has a history of seasonal allergies, and her blood pressure has been elevated.
Review of systems: The patient denies fever, shortness of breath, body aches, nausea, or vomiting.
She reports a sore throat, nasal congestion, and a cough.
Current medications: Metformin 500mg daily, hydrochlorothiazide 25mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is elevated at 144/72.
The rest of her vitals are within normal limits.
Physical exam: The patient has no frontal sinus tenderness, but has bilateral maxillary sinus tenderness.
The pharynx is erythematous, and the tonsils are symmetrical without exudates.
The patient has anterior cervical adenopathy.
The lungs are clear, and the heart exam reveals a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur at the left base.
The lower extremities show no edema.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an upper respiratory infection, likely viral in nature, causing her symptoms.
Problem list: Upper respiratory infection (suspected); type 2 diabetes (active); hypertension (active); suspected COVID-19.
Plan:
I will continue the patient on her current medications, including metformin 500mg daily and hydrochlorothiazide 25mg daily.
I will also recommend that she drink plenty of fluids and get plenty of rest to help alleviate her symptoms.
I will prescribe Coricidin HBP to help with her cough.
I will check her for COVID-19 and provide her with the results.
I will also increase her hydrochlorothiazide to 25mg daily and recommend that she continue to check her blood pressure at home several times a week.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again at her next scheduled appointment for a hemoglobin A1c check and to review her blood pressure control.
